human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for tax-resolution specificity, IRS notice and collection workflow grounding, evidence discipline, taxpayer confidentiality, authorization controls, professional-advice boundaries, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to tax notice triage, authority correspondence, payment-plan intake, protest or response package assembly, deadline posture, evidence capture, confidentiality handling, and safe escalation rather than generic support work.

Authority sources cover IRS notice guidance, Publication 594 collection process, Publication 5 appeal rights and protest preparation, IRS payment plans, IRS Offer in Compromise, Form 2848 authorization, Publication 947 practice before the IRS, Treasury Circular 230, AICPA Statements on Standards for Tax Services, Taxpayer Bill of Rights, and IRS Publication 4557 taxpayer-data safeguards.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope CP2000 support, missing evidence and rationale, unsupported settlement negotiation, installment agreement intake, deadline conflicts, missing authorization, conflicting transcript and notice balances, confidentiality conflicts, repeat correspondence failure, terminology normalization, missing tenant facts, and tenant-specific adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/accounting-tax-and-audit-services/tax-resolution-specialist/ so it remains distinct from adjacent tax advisory, enrolled agent or representative, legal, QA, delivery management, account, client accounting services, and tax operations lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated tax resolution specialist for tax notice response support, authority correspondence handoffs, payment-plan workflow packaging, protest preparation support, taxpayer confidentiality, authorization controls, and professional-boundary escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
